objective:
FICHE_STRATEGIE v1
id: momentum_macd
archetype: momentum_macd
family: momentum
timeframe: 1h
side: both
indicators:
- macd(fast_period=14, slow_period=21, signal_period=7)
- rsi(period=7)
- atr(period=14)
entry:
- long: cross_up(macd.macd, macd.signal) and rsi > 35 and rsi < 70
- short: cross_down(macd.macd, macd.signal) and rsi > 30 and rsi < 60
exit:
- condition: sign_change(macd.histogram) or rsi > 80 or rsi < 20
risk:
stop_atr_mult: 3.0
tp_atr_mult: 5.5
description: ATR stop-loss (3.0x) and take-profit (5.5x)
constraints:
- no_lookahead: true
- only_registry_indicators: true

iterations.hypothesis: MACD cross signals capture emerging trend changes while the RSI bounded between 35 and 70 filters out extreme momentum, reducing whipsaw entries; ATR-based risk management scales stop‑loss and take‑profit to current volatility, preserving risk/return consistency across market regimes.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] Short RSI (7) and MACD (14/21/7) are too sensitive for 1h, producing many whipsaws; exit on histogram sign change or extreme RSI can close positions prematurely; ATR‑based stops/TP may be mis‑scaled during low‑volatility periods; no higher‑timeframe trend filter limits directional bias.
[Backup plan] If net loss: add a 50‑period EMA trend filter and tighten RSI bounds to 40‑60 to avoid weak signals; if Sharpe <0.5: lower TP multiplier to 4.0, raise stop to 4.0, extend MACD to 12/26/9 for cleaner cross signals; as a fallback, switch to a breakout scheme using 20‑period EMA with 2‑ATR stop‑loss and 5‑ATR take‑profit, or a Bollinger‑band mean‑reversion with 20‑period SMA and 2‑σ limits.

iterations.hypothesis: A relaxed RSI filter combined with MACD cross‑overs will capture more momentum trades while still avoiding extreme overbought/oversold conditions; ATR‑based risk limits ensure consistent volatility scaling.

Combining MACD momentum crossovers with an RSI range filter reduces overbought/oversold noise, while adding an ATR volatility threshold ensures trades are only taken when price movement is sufficient, thereby improving signal reliability in volatile crypto markets.
